2605.22435.title: Assisted Counterspeech Writing at the Crossroads of Hate Speech and Misinformation
2605.22435.paper_summary: Hate speech and misinformation frequently co-occur online, amplifying prejudice and polarization. Given their scale, using Large Language Models (LLMs) to assist expert counterspeech (CS) writing has gained interest, yet prior work has addressed these phenomena separately. We bridge this gap by studying CS generation in contexts where both hate and misinformation co-occur. We test three knowledge-driven generation strategies: first we prompt an LLM with fact-checkers' guidelines and fact-checking articles; secondly, with NGOs' guidelines and reports; thirdly, we create a mixed strategy that combines guidelines and documents from both. 23 experts revise the generated CS, which are assessed via human and automatic metrics. While LLMs produce adequate CS in 40% of cases, expert edits substantially improve naturalness, exhaustiveness, and adherence to guidelines. Based on the post-edited CS, the mixed strategy proves to be the most effective in crowdsourcing evaluation, pairing strong factual correction with stereotype mitigation and empathetic engagement. We release a dataset of hateful and misinformed claims with expert-verified CS and supporting knowledge.
2605.22435.paper_summary_zh: 仇恨言論和錯誤資訊在線上經常同時出現，擴大了偏見和極化。考慮到它們的規模，使用大型語言模型（LLMs）來協助專家反駁言論（CS）寫作引起了興趣，然而之前的研究將這些現象分開處理。我們通過研究在仇恨和錯誤資訊同時出現的情境下的CS生成來填補這一空白。我們測試了三種知識驅動的生成策略：首先，我們用事實查核者的指導方針和事實查核文章來提示LLM；其次，使用非政府組織的指導方針和報告；第三，我們創建了一種混合策略，結合了來自兩者的指導方針和文件。23位專家對生成的CS進行修訂，並通過人類和自動化指標進行評估。雖然LLMs在40%的案例中生成了足夠的CS，但專家的編輯顯著提高了自然性、全面性和對指導方針的遵循。根據後編輯的CS，混合策略在眾包評估中被證明是最有效的，將強有力的事實修正與刻板印象緩解和同理心參與相結合。我們發布了一個包含仇恨和錯誤資訊主張的數據集，並附有專家驗證的CS和支持知識。
2605.22435.author: Genoveffa Martone et.al.
2605.22435.authors: Genoveffa Martone, Helena Bonaldi, Marco Guerini
